title: GLForcer
description: This Mod forces Modern OpenGl contexts supported by user drivers instead of Minecraft's default OpenGL 3.3 context.
body:
GLForcer

GLForcer is a lightweight rendering optimization mod that updates the game engine to utilize modern OpenGL contexts for better hardware efficiency.

By modernizing legacy platform constraints, GLForcer allows the game to communicate with your GPU using the highest context your hardware supports (up to OpenGL 4.6). This can result in faster geometry processing, smoother shader compilation, and a more optimized rendering pipeline.

Under the Hood
Smart Context Selection: GLForcer doesn't guess your specs. At launch, it safely probes your hardware—starting at OpenGL 4.6 and stepping down intelligently to find your GPU's exact supported version. You always get the most efficient version your machine can handle.

Fallback Protection: If you are running on older hardware that cannot handle modern 4.x contexts, the mod automatically catches the failure and falls back to the stable OpenGL 3.3 baseline used by vanilla Minecraft.

⚠️ Disclaimer
While GLForcer includes strict fallback protections and is designed to be safe for daily use, it modifies low-level window creation (GLFW). It is provided "as is," and while rare, it may cause unexpected rendering behavior on certain highly specific hardware configurations. Please report any issues on the tracker!
